message:
fix(fp-io): reflect_table avoids redundant table_schema round-trip (br-2kzv / fd90.43)

Self-review of fd90.40 surfaced a real efficiency defect:
SqlInspector::reflect_table called table_schema explicitly, then
called primary_key_columns which (via fd90.25's default impl)
calls table_schema AGAIN. For backends where each call is a
network round-trip (real PG/MySQL impls), that's a doubled cost
on what's documented as a 'one bundle, one fetch' API.

Fix: derive the primary-key column list directly from the
SqlTableSchema we already fetched. New free-fn
primary_keys_from_schema(meta) replicates the default impl's
filter+sort logic on already-fetched metadata. Pure efficiency
fix — no behavior change visible to callers.

New test sql_inspector_reflect_table_calls_table_schema_only_once
uses a Cell-counting stub that verifies exactly one table_schema
invocation across the whole reflect_table call. Asserts the bundle
also got primary_key_columns=['id'] correctly to confirm the
derivation is functionally equivalent to the trait dispatch.

411 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean across fp-io +
umbrella; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
